repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
contents_tesim: This is a Judeo-Arabic, probably Karaite translation of North African origin, in Arabic script. The translation was clearly made from a Hebrew original. The text uses the "Maghrebine" form of 1 sg. imperfect (with the n- prefix): e.g., fols. 7v and 12r; the letter /f/ sometimes has a dot underneath. The translation is different from both Saadia and Yefet
full_text_tesim: The Arabic undertext runs at a right angle to the overtext
The full number of lines is not always preserved

This is a Judeo-Arabic, probably Karaite translation of North African origin, in Arabic script. The translation was clearly made from a Hebrew original. The text uses the "Maghrebine" form of 1 sg. imperfect (with the n- prefix): e.g., fols. 7v and 12r; the letter /f/ sometimes has a dot underneath. The translation is different from both Saadia and Yefet
